course_title: Spatial and Quantitative Methods for Transport Data Analytics

learning_outcomes.description: Identify, assess, and address common data issues including data types, missing values, noise, and outliers
Perform exploratory data analysis to summarise data and uncover meaningful patterns and relationships
Develop and interpret regression models for inference and prediction
Analyse spatial data and account for spatial structure in data-driven analysis

Report and present resultApply clustering and data reduction techniques to identify structure in high-dimensional datasetss of transport data analytics to decision makers working on transport solutions.
Select, implement, and compare machine learning models for prediction and pattern recognition
Effectively interpret the results and communicate outcomes to the general public as well as experts in the field using a variety of professional communication styles

assessments.category: Computer Code, Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
The first project will involve the description, analysis, manipulation, visualisation, and reporting of results, using the tools of linear regression. For CIVL7415 students, additional tasks and a critical analysis will be required in this assessment item, above the assessment activities for CIVL3530 students.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All electronically submitted assessment items must be submitted through TurnItIn in BlackBoard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.category: Computer Code, Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Tutorial/ Problem Set
assessments.task_description:
The second project will involve the description, analysis, manipulation, visualisation, and reporting of results, using data-driven tools such as GIS and machine learning. For CIVL7415 students, additional tasks and a critical analysis will be required in this assessment item, above the assessment activities for CIVL3530 students.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: All electronically submitted assessment items must be submitted through Turnitin, in BlackBoard.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
You are expected to actively participate in lectures throughout the semester. In-class quizzes will contribute to your final marks, with points accruing from Week 1 through to Week 13. The full marks for each quiz are 1.0, and your final score will be calculated based on your best 10 out of 13 quizzes. To ensure your quiz is counted, it must be submitted via Blackboard before the end of each lecture.

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
A final examination will be held during the standard examination period at the end of the semester. This exam will consist of short answer and problem-solving questions. For CIVL7415 students, additional questions will be required in this assessment item, above the assessment activities for CIVL3530 students.

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

Tutorial Activities

Tutorials will be conducted in the one-hour sessions and will focus on practical applications using software and real-world data sets.
